2.4:
\nl< >"The weather doesn’t look too good today,
so be careful, okay…"
2.5:
\nl< >"If you feel like you can’t hold on, 
make sure to come back right away!"
2.6:
Seeing \str[120] looking so full of energy in the morning,
such a rare sight instantly filled me with deep emotion.
2.7:
Suppressing the urge to tightly hug \str[120], 
I simply smiled back at her.
2.9: "Alright… Be good and look after the house, okay?"
2.10:
I waved to \str[120] as I left home,
reminding myself I must work hard again today—for her sake!
2.11:
\nl< >"I’m sorry, \str[121]…    \w[60]
I’m making you go to such a dangerous place again—"
2.12: "There you go again. Don’t worry, it’ll be fine."
2.14:
"I’ve already explored it many times,
and for years that labyrinth has remained completely stable, 
so it’s fine."
2.15: "Alright… Be good and look after the house, okay?"
2.17: Yeah. \str[120]… she suffers from a strange illness.
2.18:
She cannot go outside, nor can she be exposed to sunlight. 
And if she doesn’t periodically take a specially crafted alchemic medicine, 
her condition supposedly becomes even more unstable…
2.19:
The ingredients for this medicine aren’t something 
you can simply buy on the market. 
The only place left where I can possibly gather them all is there—
2.20:
So as her family, no matter how many times it takes, 
I must venture into that place to find a way to 
cure \str[120] once and for all.
3.2:
"Yeah, I felt a bit tired today,
so I didn’t push myself to go exploring…"
3.3: \nl< >"This is that thing… called May Sickness, right?"
3.4: May…? But it’s the middle of summer now.
3.5:
\nl< >"I often feel the same way…
I thought it was still noon, but when I woke up, 
\str[121] had already made dinner…"
3.7:
\nl< >"It’s nice to take it easy sometimes, \str[121]. 
Come play with me!"
3.8:
\nl< >"Getting proper rest is important too, 
so be mindful next time."
3.9:
\nl< >"I see,
then let me give you a massage, \str[121]!"
3.10: Yeah, there’s plenty of time—let’s just relax and rest today.
3.11: You’re right, there’s plenty of time—let’s just relax and rest today.
3.12:
\nl< >"Then why don’t you try napping too, \str[121]…
it feels really good, you know?"
3.13: In the end, I napped with \str[120] until the sun went down.
3.14: I spent the whole day playing games with \str[120].
3.15: I spent a relaxing day with \str[120].
3.16:
\str[120] enthusiastically gave me a massage for a long time,
and because it felt so nice, I drifted off to sleep…
3.19:
"Hehehe, hey \str[120]—
guess what I brought home today—!"
3.22:
Yeah… looks like \str[120] really has no interest 
in anything other than food…
3.26:
\nl< >"\str[121], you reek!   \w[90]
Don’t come near me…               \w[90]
Go take a bath already!           \w[150]\^
3.28:
\str[120] ran away.     \w[60]
…Do I really smell that bad? 
Did I pick up something nasty while exploring?
3.29: Anyway, I should hurry and take a shower first…
4.1:
I expected things to be like usual, 
with \str[120] running to greet me with a healing smile.
But as soon as I return, I see her standing in the entryway 
with her hands on her hips.
Her delicate little face is masked in undisguised resentment, 
like a judge waiting for a criminal to be brought to justice.
4.2:
Judging by her motionless stance,
she has clearly been maintaining this pose 
and waiting here for me for a long time.
4.3:
That's strange. Have I done anything lately 
to make \str[120] angry...?
I quickly mentally review my recent actions, 
but I can't find any clues.
4.5:
Oh no! Even \str[120]'s voice is ice-cold! \{
She is definitely pissed off!?
4.6:
Instant Surrender
Play innocent
Tentatively probe
4.7:
Countless 'crime scenes' flash through my mind in an instant.
Was it because I stole and drank her milk the other day?
Or was it because I pinched her cheeks while she was napping?
Or maybe she's holding a grudge about me peeking at her in the bathroom!?
4.8:
The more I think about it, the more I feel like I'm in deep trouble.
I quickly make the judgment and execute the most appropriate reaction—
4.10:
I lunged forward and performed a textbook Dogeza before my \str[120].
In a situation like this, showing sincere remorse by prostrating myself 
is the easiest way to win her forgiveness...
4.11:
"Whether it's the milk or those  pranks...
Please, just show some mercy with the punishment!!"
4.12:
\nl< >"Eh!? W-wait a minute...
\str[121]... what on earth are you talking about?"
4.13:
My majestic \str[120] was startled back half 
a step by my exaggerated posture.
Even the resentment on her face dissipated by half due 
to sheer shock.
4.14: "—Wait, so you're not angry about those things...?"
4.15:
\nl< >"Of course not—!
I mean, I am angry, but not because of that!"
4.16:
\nl< >"It's about this...!
I found these under your bed while 
I was cleaning the room today!!"
4.16.5:
\nl< >"I noticed before that 
you were secretly collecting these things...
But the pile just keeps growing!
You have some explaining to do!"
4.17:
\str[120] stepped aside to reveal a large cardboard box 
filled with various items.
Because I was so focused on her, I hadn't noticed 
the box's existence at all until now.
4.18: "Ah, it's just the stuff I got from the Gacha machines..."
4.19:
\nl< >"That's exactly it! The collection just keeps growing!
\str[121], have you been spending a bit too much money lately?"
4.20:
Being questioned like that by \str[120] made me feel guilty.
I could only keep my head down while remaining in the Dogeza position.
4.21:
\nl< >"Even though it's money you earned yourself,
you still need to be careful about how you spend it...!
The money you earn from fighting so hard should be used properly!"
4.23:
Listening to my \str[120]'s caring lecture,
I could only bow my head and agree, 
not daring to offer a single word of rebuttal.
 
In this narrow entryway, 
her small figure seemed incredibly tall and reliable at this moment.
4.24: "But... when it comes to Gacha... I can't stop my hands...!"
4.25:
It's the truth. 
Every time I start a Gacha machine, 
the sensation in my fingertips feels like magic.
 
That spell called 'The next pull is the one' 
always makes me lose total control.
By the time I snap out of it, my wallet is already empty.
4.26:
\nl< >"...Phew. I figured you'd say that...
Since you can't control your own hands, 
you should just let me keep the money for you!"
4.27:
\nl< >"It's not like I can use this money anyway...
If there's something you really want to buy, 
you can just ask me for it!"
4.28:
Hearing this proposal, I was stunned for a moment, 
but then a strange warmth welled up in my heart.
Thinking about it, because of her physical condition, 
she can never go out, so she's never received an allowance.
4.29:
Maybe doing this doesn't strictly have a practical meaning,
but that's no reason not to let her feel special.
4.30: "Yeah... that would actually be a huge help."
4.31:
\nl< >"Hehe...
Since you trust me that much, 
I'll be sure to keep track of every single cent!"
4.32:
\nl< >"Lastly... 
you're going to explain exactly what happened with 
my milk later..."
4.34:
\{\c[2]
You can now give \str[120] an allowance!
5.0:
"N-no more…   \w[30]
I almost died out there…"
5.1:
It was lucky I managed to escape at the last moment…
As I thought, with my current strength it’s still way too reckless 
to challenge that place…
5.2:
I should train more, fully prepare myself,
and only then think about conquering it…
5.5:
\nl< >"\str[121], are you alright!?    \w[60]
You’re covered in wounds…!"
5.6:
"Ahaha… I pushed myself a little too hard,
and ended up failing…"
5.7:
\nl< >"\str[121], honestly…
you really need to take better care of yourself!"
5.8: \nl< >"Since you’re hurt, we need to bandage you up quickly!"
5.9:
\str[120] skillfully tended to my wounds, 
and I felt a great deal of strength return.
5.10:
\str[120] carefully bandaged me with clumsy hands, 
and I felt somewhat recovered.
5.11:
\str[120] clumsily wrapped me head to toe in bandages, 
and I felt just a little bit better.
5.12:
\nl< >"Now, \str[121], you just rest up!
Leave dinner to me!"
5.14:
That said, my whole body’s wrapped like a mummy, 
and I can’t move at all…
5.16:
Was it just my imagination, 
or did \str[120] seem more motivated than usual…?
5.17: \nl< >"\str[121], you pushed yourself too far again!"
5.18: \nl< >"We have to stop the bleeding quickly!"
5.19:
\nl< >"From now on, 
let me take care of you, \str[121]!"
5.20:
Was it just my imagination, 
or did \str[120] look even more excited than usual…?
9.0:
The warmth in my arms reminded me that 
I had finally obtained the medicine I longed for. 
Yet the surreal nature of this experience still left me unsettled.
9.1:
The Star tarot card at the entrance connected.
It was one of the belongings left behind by my father.
Although he had spent his life researching a way to cure \str[120],
why did it react so easily now…?
9.2:
No matter how I thought about it, I couldn’t reach a conclusion.
Overthinking was meaningless. 
The important thing is—I now have the Elixir Rubrum.
I should find a chance to let \str[120] try it.
9.3:
Its color even looks like strawberry juice,
maybe it even tastes like her favorite strawberry milk…
9.6: "Alright, alright, I’ll get dinner ready right away."
9.7:
I gently patted \str[120]’s head and comforted her a little. 
Alright—what should I do next?
9.8:
Prepare dinner
Go to the toilet
9.9: Yeah, it’s getting late, I’d better go prepare dinner.
9.10: "What would you like to eat tonight?"
9.12:
Hmm… just as expected…
Looks like I really need to find a chance to explain to 
\str[120] how important a balanced diet is.
9.13: Ugh, I kind of feel like going to the toilet…
9.14: "Sorry, just wait a bit, I’ll prepare dinner afterward."
13.0:
\nl< >"\str[121], you didn’t come back all beaten up today 
just like we promised, great job! 
You deserve some praise for working so hard!"
13.1:
\nl< >"\str[121], you kept our promise again today. 
Here’s today’s reward!"
13.2:
\nl< >"\str[121], you’ve been working hard lately. 
Here’s today’s reward—"
13.3:
\nl< >"It’s almost been a month now. You’ve done so well,
\str[121]. Come on, here’s today’s reward—"
13.4:
\nl< >"\str[121]! Wonderful, it’s already been a month! 
You’re amazing, \str[121]!"
13.6:
\nl< >"Alright! Done drawing!
This is proof that you’ve been such a good kid, \str[121]! 
You’re not allowed to wash it off today!"
13.7:
I looked at my right hand, where \str[120] had drawn 
a cute little picture on the back with her brush.
13.8: What an incredible kind of reward… but still…
13.9:
\nl< >"Eh? 
‘Will there be some special reward if I keep collecting them?’
…\str[121], do you want even more praise?"
13.10:
\nl< >"Hmm… well, if you keep this up for another month,
I’ll give you an even more special reward…"
13.11:
\nl< >"But… it’s too embarrassing, 
so I can’t tell you what it is just yet…"
13.12:
I was only joking, but \str[120] seemed to be 
seriously thinking about some kind of special reward. 
It actually made me feel motivated!
13.13:
\nl< >"The special reward is still a secret. 
I can’t tell you now, \str[121]…!"
13.14:
\nl< >"Here you go, \str[121]! 
Just as promised, this is your special reward!"
13.15: \nl< >"Hehe… it’s a ‘Do Anything’ coupon I made."
13.16:
\nl< >"\str[121], you’re always thinking about me, but…
I also want you to be able to put down those burdens and take it easy…"
13.17:
\nl< >"So, you can use this to pamper yourself 
with me as much as you like!"
Any request is fine—just focus on relaxing yourself!"
13.18:
\nl< >"Eh!?     \w[30]
‘Even… lewd things would be okay…?’
Um, um…"
13.19:
\nl< >"It’s fine… if that’s what you really want,
I won’t refuse…"
13.20:
\nl< >"After all, \str[121], 
you’re always teasing me anyway…"
13.21:
\>Received \ii[199] from \str[120]!                   \w[60]
\>You can now make a spoiled request to \str[120]!     \w[60]
14.1:
"Ah… today I just received this outfit from a knight,
he said it was a game bonus—"          \w[30]\^
14.2:
\nl< >"The knight from 'Shadow Soul'!? 
Quick, let me see—"
14.3:
\str[120] darted in front of me at lightning speed 
and snatched the clothes—
Huh… looks like she really likes this gift.
14.4: \nl< >"Ooooh—! There’s even the knight’s autograph on it!"
14.6:
\{"Ah, wait—!? 
We’re still at the entrance!"
14.7:
I hurriedly turned away and quickly shut the front door. 
\str[120]’s skin can’t withstand long exposure to the sun. 
Was she so excited that she forgot her own weakness!?
14.10:
That knight insisted on always wearing heavy armor, 
so I assumed he must have been tall and broad. 
I worried \str[120] wouldn’t be able to wear it properly, 
but to my surprise, the size fit her perfectly…
14.11: \nl< >"Ehehe… I’ll just use this as pajamas from now on~"
14.12:
"That’ll wrinkle it… 
isn’t it supposed to be a collector’s item…"
14.13:
"Also, it’s almost time to eat. 
Hurry and change out of it first."
15.1:
I greeted weakly,
and as soon as I stepped inside, 
the burning heat all over my body instantly faded away.
Damn weather… to think it could get this hot…
15.3:
I realized \str[120] didn’t come to greet me like usual. 
Is she still asleep?
15.5:
Wearing just a T-shirt, 
\str[120] walked out sluggishly to greet me,
it was obvious the heat had worn her down too…
15.8:
Looking down at my body still steaming with heat,
for a moment it even felt like I might explode…
15.9:
\nl< >"\str[121], welcome home…    \w[60]
It must’ve been really hot outside, huh?"
15.10:
"Yeah, it felt like being inside an oven…   \w[60]
What the heck is with this weather…"
15.11:
"But it’s all fine now!!    \w[40]
Ta-da! I got us a fan!"
15.12:
\nl< >"That’s great! Hurry, hurry!   \w[60]
Set it up right now!"
15.13: \nl< >"I’ve got the fan and popsicles ready, come on!"
15.15:
Coming home to a cool place with popsicles to eat,
this feeling isn’t so bad at all…
15.16:
After waiting a while, \str[120] still didn’t come out.
Strange… could she still be napping?
15.19:
Alright! I’ll just quietly sneak to her room,
it’d be a shame if I woke her up while she’s napping.
15.21:
I called out casually,
and before long I could hear 
hurried footsteps coming downstairs.
15.24:
Probably because she came running,
\str[120]’s cheeks were flushed and her 
breathing was unusually quick and uneven.
15.27:
"You were secretly eating chips just now, weren’t you!?
Look, there are crumbs at the corner of your mouth!"
15.28:
\nl< >"Eh… ah… \w[45] umm… yeah, that’s right,    \w[45]
ehehe… I was snacking a little…"                 \w[45]
15.29:
\str[120] fumbled awkwardly with her excuse, 
and it just looked more suspicious…
15.30:
\nl< >"Alright, alright, I’ve got a portion for you too,
Let’s eat together…!"
15.31:
Not giving me a chance to question her further, 
\str[120] slipped away.
15.32:
Back at the entrance, my mind kept 
replaying the image of Imouto’s careless, revealing figure.     \w[60]
…Well, it is summer after all.
15.34:
I pretended like I hadn’t noticed anything 
as I called out to \str[120] inside.
